item.title: Labor Day and Abaco Investigation
item.ns0:title: Thousands March in Solidarity for Annual Sir Randol Fawkes Labor Day Parade — Friday, June 5th, 2026
item.description:
Tonight on Our News, thousands take to the streets for a historic Labor Day celebration as union leaders reflect on the legacy of Sir Randol Fawkes and the ongoing struggle for workers' rights. We provide a major update on the international search in Abaco for a missing American woman as the investigation shifts from a missing persons case to a suspected murder. Our team also investigates a string of recent violence in the capital and examines the "brain drain" crisis discussed at the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting. Plus, stay tuned for your complete holiday weekend weather forecast and a recap of the top plays from the world of sports.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.

Tonight on Our News, thousands take to the streets for a historic Labor Day celebration as union leaders reflect on the legacy of Sir Randol Fawkes and the ongoing struggle for workers' rights. We provide a major update on the international search in Abaco for a missing American woman as the investigation shifts from a missing persons case to a suspected murder. Our team also investigates a string of recent violence in the capital and examines the "brain drain" crisis discussed at the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting. Plus, stay tuned for your complete holiday weekend weather forecast and a recap of the top plays from the world of sports.

Tonight on Our News, thousands take to the streets for a historic Labor Day celebration as union leaders reflect on the legacy of Sir Randol Fawkes and the ongoing struggle for workers' rights. We provide a major update on the international search in Abaco for a missing American woman as the investigation shifts from a missing persons case to a suspected murder. Our team also investigates a string of recent violence in the capital and examines the "brain drain" crisis discussed at the Caribbean Development Bank’s annual meeting.
